assertions.text: I was traveling at about 90 km/h prior to the collision.
I was traveling at highway speed before impact.
I struck the back of a parked vehicle with my front bumper (front-to-rear impact).
The other vehicle was parked (stationary) at the time of impact.
I hit the brakes only at the last moment before impact.
Braking occurred after most of the approach and immediately before the collision (i.e., braking began at the last moment before impact).
There was a single impact (struck the back of the parked vehicle once).
The collision involved the front bumper of my vehicle impacting the rear of the parked vehicle (implying a frontal-to-rear bumper collision, not described as minor or severe).

verdicts.evidence: The claimant said they were traveling about [90 km/h](T1) prior to collision, but the EDR pre-crash speed samples show speeds around [34.0 km/h] to [32.0 km/h] with a first value of [34.0 km/h] at t=[-5.0 s] and last pre-impact value [32.0 km/h] at t=[0.0 s] (T2,T3). The recorded speeds (max [36.0 km/h]) contradict the [90 km/h](T1) claim (T2,T3).

The claim of "highway speed" is contradicted by the recorded pre-crash speeds: the EDR shows first_value [34.0 km/h] and last_value [32.0 km/h], with a max of [36.0 km/h] over the pre-crash window (T2,T3), well below typical highway speeds and the vehicle info speed limit [48.0 km/h] (T1).

The crash pulse shows a negative longitudinal delta-V with final cumulative delta-V_long of [‑18.0 km/h] at t=[0.16 s], indicating the vehicle experienced a decelerating forward-directed impact consistent with a front-to-rear impact (T3,T1).

Whether the other vehicle was stationary cannot be determined from V1's sensors alone; V1's recorded speed samples are [34.0 km/h] first and [32.0 km/h] last, and delta-V_long final is [‑18.0 km/h], but there is no measurement of the other vehicle's motion in the data (T2,T3).

Brake channel transitions show the brake was off until a transition at [‑0.5 s] from 0 to 1 and remained on at impact (last_value [1] at t=[0.0 s]), so braking began only shortly before impact, supporting the claim that braking occurred at the last moment (T2,T3).

The brake transition occurred at [‑0.5 s] (from 0 to 1) while speed changed only from [35.0 km/h] at t=[‑0.5 s] to [32.0 km/h] at t=[0.0 s], showing braking began immediately before impact rather than earlier (T2,T3).
The EDR summary indicates [num_events_recorded: 1] and the delta-V_long crash pulse shows a single cumulative event with final delta-V_long of [‑18.0 km/h], consistent with a single impact (T1,T3).

Telemetry shows a forward-facing deceleration pulse with cumulative longitudinal delta-V of [‑18.0 km/h] and minimal lateral delta-V (peak [‑1.0 km/h]), indicating the vehicle's front took the impact consistent with a frontal-to-rear bumper collision (T3,T1).

result.rationale: Telemetry shows V1 traveling at about [32.0–36.0 km/h] pre-impact, braking initiated [‑0.5 s] before impact, and a single decelerating crash pulse with cumulative longitudinal delta-V of [‑18.0 km/h], consistent with a front-to-rear collision and a single impact (T2,T3,T1).

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
